rulings.Case Title: GENE LEPORE VS. AC AND S INC et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 11, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Ford Motor Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Lepore was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HARVEY NULL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Renewal Of Summary Judgment Motion
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 11, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Schneider Electric USA Inc.¿s motion for renewal of motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that new or different facts, circumstances or law exist as required by C.C.P. section 1008(b). Casey v. Perini Corp. is inapplicable to the facts here. The court declines to exercise its inherent authority to sua sponte reconsider its prior order. Plaintiff¿s request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY HARRIS VS. AMCORD INC. et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO KERN COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue And Stay Proceedings Pending Transfer
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 11, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc.¿s motion to transfer venue to the Superior Court for the County of Kern and stay proceedings pending transfer is granted. rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY HARRIS VS. AMCORD INC. et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO KERN COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Defendant Kelly- Moore Paint Company, Inc.¿S Motion To Transfer Venue And Stay Proceedings Pending Transfer
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 11, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Kern Building Materials, Inc.¿s joinder to Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc.¿s motion to transfer venue to the Superior Court for the County of Kern and stay proceedings pending transfer is off calendar as moot given the ruling on the underlying motion. = (503/TLJ)
